commit.message:
Add `c4 install` command for one-command setup

Embed all .c4/ templates as heredocs inside c4.sh so the script is
fully self-contained. Running `c4 install [path]` bootstraps the
entire .c4/ structure and copies c4.sh into the target folder —
no manual file copying required.

- Add cmd_install with embedded AGENTS.md, roster.md, config, all
  ROLE.md and PROFILE.md templates, and events log
- Guard against installing into an already-initialised folder
- Update cmd_help to document the new command
- Update README Quick Start and Install Globally sections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace curl upload with Python3 streaming to fix OOM and 403

curl -T -X POST sends an 'Expect: 100-continue' header which some servers
(including Harvester via Rancher proxy) reject with 403 before the body
is even sent. curl --data-binary buffers the entire file in RAM (OOM on
large images).

Replace the binary upload in _do_upload() with Python3 (http.client):
- Streams in 4 MB chunks via conn.send() — no in-memory buffering
- No Expect: 100-continue header sent
- Shows percentage, MB, and MB/s progress on stderr
- Prints the server error body on failure for easier diagnosis

Python3 is already required by _kc_field(), so no new dependency.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Updated baseline.json and post.json with the latest code analysis metrics
reflecting changes to internal/tls package structure and functions.

commit.message:
Fix raytracer proxy cylinder width inflated by Scale nodes (#354)

* Initial plan

* Fix: scale-compensate proxy cylinder radii in raytracer scene collector

In SoRaytracerSceneCollector::computeWorldSpaceRadius, the returned
radius was used as a local-space value in proxy geometry (createCylinderProxy
etc.), but src_collectProxy wraps every proxy with the full model matrix,
which inflated the radius by any Scale node factor.  For the CenterballManip
feedback arrows (Scale 3 5 1.05), this made the proxy cylinders 3-5x too
wide in NanoRT/Embree views.

Fix: divide the computed radius by cbrtf(sx*sy*sz) – the geometric mean
of the three row-vector lengths of the model matrix – so the net world-space
radius after model-matrix application equals the pixel-size target.  For
pure rotation/translation (scale==1) the division is a no-op.

Apply the same fix to SoProceduralShape.cpp's non-GL wireframe path.

Add a scale-compensation pixel-count validation to the NanoRT proxy shapes
rendering test to guard against future regressions.

commit.message:
fix(wasm): directly strip concurrent/opengl/svg from QWT .pro files

Two-strategy approach to ensure wasm_singlethread-unavailable Qt modules
are never added to QT by QWT's build system:

1. Append QWT_CONFIG -= <feat> to qwtconfig.pri (existing approach,
   belt-and-suspenders).
2. NEW: use find+xargs+sed to remove 'concurrent', 'opengl', and 'svg'
   from every QT += line in every .pro and .pri file in the QWT tree.
   This is guaranteed to work even if qmake sub-project re-evaluation
   re-reads qwtconfig.pri in a way that bypasses our -= overrides.

Fix race detector build failure in loader package

tried to set an undefined variable in init().

reducing complexity.

- Metrics: Removed init() function (complexity 1.3 → 0)

intent_classifier.py had a comprehensive inline system_prompt string while
intent_classifier.md was outdated (missing INVENTORY, WORLD_BUILDING,
META_FEEDBACK, OVERRIDE_COMMAND, OP_COMMAND intents). Updated the .md with

profile_merge.py had MERGE_ANALYSIS_PROMPT and MERGE_EXECUTION_PROMPT as
inline Python string constants. The single profile_merge.md in the registry
couldn't represent both phases. Split into profile_merge_analysis.md and

commit.message:
fix: use curl -T for streaming upload instead of --data-binary

--data-binary (even with @-/stdin) buffers the full payload in memory
before sending, causing OOM on large images.

-T (--upload-file) streams the file directly using the on-disk file
size for Content-Length, avoiding any in-memory buffering.
Combined with -X POST to keep the HTTP method correct.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
